title: hordeum jubatum subsp. intermedium
intro: Hordeum jubatum subsp. intermedium, also known as blue grama grass, is a type of grass native to North America and is characterized by its blue-green foliage and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hordeum jubatum subsp. intermedium include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as possibly alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the dried flowers or leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's essential oil in aromatherapy.
